mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the predictive coefficient flips positive in a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, higher discount rates compress equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a 3-year rolling out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 53%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, supply-side capacity utilization improves and margin expansion accelerates, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in a 2-year walk-forward test.
